Name: Ampharos
Pok\u00e9-BODY-Effect: Treat each Basic Pokémon and Evolution card in your deck, in your discard pile, in your hand, and in play as a Pokémon that has δ on its card.
Effects: Does 20 damage plus 10 more damage for each Pokémon you have in play that has δ on its card.

Name: Feraligatr
Pok\u00e9-BODY-Effect: Each of your Pokémon that has δ on its card does 10 more damage to the Defending Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Effects: Before doing damage, you may choose 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon and switch it with 1 of the Defending Pokémon. Your opponent chooses the Defending Pokémon to switch.

Name: Heracross
Pok\u00e9-BODY-Effect: As long as Heracross is the only Pokémon you have in play, your opponent’s Basic Pokémon can’t attack.
Effects: Search your discard pile for an Energy card, show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand.
If the Defending Pokémon is Pokémon-ex, this attack does 30 damage plus 20 more damage.

Name: Meganium
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn, when you play Meganium from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon, you may search your deck for up to 3 in any combination of Basic Pokémon or Evolution cards. Show them to your opponent and put them into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, any damage done by attacks from the Defending Pokémon is reduced by 30 (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

Name: Milotic
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may look at your opponent’s hand. You may use the effect of a Supporter card you find there as the effect of this power. (The Supporter card remains in your opponent’s hand.) You can’t use more than 1 Sharing Poké-Power each turn. This power can’t be used if Milotic is affected by a Special Condition.

Name: Nidoking
Effects: Choose 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon. This attack does 30 damage to that Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

You may discard a Basic Pokémon or Evolution card from your hand. If you do, choose 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon and do 20 damage to that Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

Name: Nidoqueen
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may search your deck for a Basic Pokémon or Evolution card, show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward. This power can’t be used if Nidoqueen is affected by a Special Condition.
Effects: Does 30 damage plus 10 more damage for each Basic Pokémon and each Evolution card in your discard pile. You can’t add more than 60 damage in this way.

Name: Ninetales
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may remove 4 damage counters from Ninetales and discard Ninetales from Vulpix. If you do, search your deck for Ninetales or Ninetales ex and put it onto Vulpix (this counts as evolving Vulpix). Shuffle your deck afterward.
Effects: Search your discard pile for an Energy card and attach it to 1 of your Pokémon.

Name: Pinsir
Pok\u00e9-BODY-Effect: If your opponent has 5 of more cards in his or her hand, any damage done to Pinsir by attacks is reduced by 30 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Effects: Search your deck for a Pokémon that has δ on its card, show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

Name: Snorlax
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), if Snorlax is your Active Pokémon, you may remove 2 damage counters from Snorlax and Snorlax is now Asleep. This power can’t be used if Snorlax is affected by a Special Condition.
Move-Energy:
Poké-BODY ⇢ Bedhead
As long as Snorlax remains Asleep between turns, put 2 damage counters on 1 of the Defending Pokémon.

Name: Togetic
Effects: Choose an attack on 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon in play that has δ on its card. Delta Copy copies that attack except for its Energy cost. (You must still do anything else required for that attack.) Togetic performs that attack.

Name: Typhlosion
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), if Typhlosion is your Active Pokémon, you may move 1 damage counter from either player’s Pokémon to another Pokémon (yours or your opponent’s). This power can’t be used if Typhlosion is affected by a Special Condition.
Effects: If Typhlosion has at least 2 [R] Energy attached to it, the Defending Pokémon is now Burned.

Name: Arbok
Effects: The Defending Pokémon is now Burned and Poisoned.
If the Defending Pokémon has δ on its card, this attack does 50 damage plus 30 more damage.

Name: Cloyster
Pok\u00e9-BODY-Effect: Prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done by your opponent’s Pokémon to each of your Benched Pokémon that has δ on its card.
Effects: Does 10 damage plus 10 more damage for each Energy attached to Cloyster.

Name: Dewgong
Pok\u00e9-BODY-Effect: Any damage done to Dewgong by attacks from your opponent’s Pokémon that has δ on its card is reduced by 40 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
If Dewgong has at least 2 [W] Energy attached to it, this attack does 40 damage plus 20 more damage.

Name: Gligar
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed and switch Gligar with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
If the Defending Pokémon is Pokémon-ex, this attack does 10 damage plus 10 more damage and the Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

Name: Jynx
Pok\u00e9-BODY-Effect: As long as Jynx is an Evolved Pokémon, you pay [C] less to retreat your [R] and [P] Pokémon.

Name: Ledian
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn, when you play Ledian from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon, you may search your deck for any 1 card and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Effects: If Ledian has a Pokémon Tool card attached to it, draw 3 cards.

Name: Lickitung
Effects: Draw 2 cards.
Put 1 damage counter on 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon. If that Pokémon has δ on its card, put 3 damage counters instead.

Name: Mantine
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may search your discard pile for a basic Energy card, show it to your opponent, and put it on top of your deck. If you do, put 1 damage counter on Mantine. This power can’t be used if Mantine is affected by a Special Condition.
Effects: Remove 1 damage counter from Mantine.

Name: Quagsire
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn, when you play Quagsire from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon, you may search your discard pile for up to 2 Pokémon Tool cards, show them to your opponent, and put them into your hand.
Effects: If Quagsire has a Pokémon Tool card attached to it, this attack does 50 damage plus 20 more damage.

Name: Seadra
Effects: If the Defending Pokémon tries to attack during your opponent’s next turn, your opponent flips a coin. If tails, that attack does nothing.

Name: Tropius
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn, when you put Tropius from your hand onto your Bench, you may remove all Special Conditions, Imprison markers, and Shock-wave markers from your Pokémon.
Effects: Does 10 damage times the amount of Energy attached to Tropius.

Name: Vibrava
Pok\u00e9-BODY-Effect: If Vibrava has any [P] Energy attached to it, the Retreat Cost for Vibrava is 0.
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 damage plus 20 more damage.

Name: Xatu
Pok\u00e9-BODY-Effect: Each of your Stage 2 Pokémon-ex does 10 more damage to the Defending Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

Name: Bayleef
Effects: The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

Name: Croconaw
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon can’t attack or retreat during your opponent’s next turn.

Name: Dragonair
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

Name: Electabuzz
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), if Electabuzz is an Evolved Pokémon, you may draw a card from the bottom of your deck. This power can’t be used if Electabuzz is affected by a Special Condition.
Effects: This attack’s damage isn’t affected by Weakness, Resistance, Poké-Powers, Poké-Bodies, or any other effects on the Defending Pokémon.

Name: Flaaffy
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

Name: Horsea
Effects: Switch Horsea with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

Name: Kirlia
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
If Kirlia and the Defending Pokémon have a different amount of Energy attached to them, this attack’s base damage is 30 instead of 60.

Name: Kirlia
Effects: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

Name: Nidorina
Effects: The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

Name: Nidorino
Effects: Does 10 damage plus 10 more damage for each damage counter on Nidorino.

Name: Quilava
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 damage plus 20 more damage.

Name: Seadra
Effects: If the Defending Pokémon is a Pokémon-ex, this attack does 30 damage plus 30 more damage.

Name: Shelgon
Effects: Shelgon does 10 damage to itself.

Name: Smeargle
Effects: Draw a card.
If the Defending Pokémon is Pokémon-ex, this attack does 10 damage plus 10 more damage and the Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

Name: Swellow
Pok\u00e9-BODY-Effect: The Retreat Cost for each of your Stage 2 Pokémon-ex is 0.
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all effects of an attack, including damage, done to Swellow during your opponent’s next turn.

Name: Togepi
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, any damage done by attacks from the Defending Pokémon is reduced by 20 (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

Name: Vibrava
Effects: If the Defending Pokémon is Pokémon-ex, that Pokémon is now Confused.

Name: Bagon
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, any damage done to Bagon by attacks is reduced by 10 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

Name: Chikorita
Effects: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

Name: Cyndaquil
Effects: This attack’s damage isn’t affected by Weakness, Resistance, Poké-Powers, Poké-Bodies, or any other effects on that Pokémon.

Name: Elekid
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may put Electabuzz from your hand onto Elekid (this counts as evolving Elekid) and remove all damage counters from Elekid.
Effects: Choose 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon. This attack does 10 damage to that Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

Name: Feebas
Effects: Does 10 damage times the number of damage counters on Feebas.

Name: Horsea
Effects: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

Name: Larvitar
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

Name: Ledyba
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

Name: Nidoran Female
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon can’t attack during your opponent’s next turn.

Name: Nidoran Male
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

Name: Pupitar
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy card attached to the Defending Pokémon.

Name: Ralts
Effects: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.
Does 10 damage times the amount of Energy attached to the Defending Pokémon.

Name: Ralts
Effects: Remove 2 damage counters from Ralts.

Name: Shellder
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

Name: Smoochum
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may put Jynx from your hand onto Smoochum (this counts as evolving Smoochum) and remove all damage counters from Smoochum.
Effects: Search your deck for a Basic Pokémon and basic Energy card, show them to your opponent, and put them into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

Name: Swablu
Effects: If Swablu has less Energy attached to it than the Defending Pokémon, this attack does 10 damage plus 10 more damage.

Name: Taillow
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 damage plus 10 more damage.

Name: Totodile
Effects: Does 10 damage plus 10 more damage for each damage counter on Totodile.

Name: Trapinch
Effects: Choose 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon. This attack does 10 damage to that Pokémon. This attack’s damage isn’t affected by Weakness, Resistance, Poké-Powers, Poké-Bodies, or any other effects on that Pokémon.

Name: Vulpix
Effects: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

Name: Wooper
Effects: Choose 1 of the Defending Pokémon’s attacks. That Pokémon can’t use that attack during your opponent’s next turn.

Name: Buffer Piece
Description: Damage done to the Pokémon Buffer Piece is attached to by an opponent’s attack is reduced by 20 (after applying Weakness and Resistance). At the end of your opponent’s turn after you played Buffer Piece, discard Buffer Piece.
Tool-Rule: Attach Buffer Piece to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn’t already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it. If that Pokémon is Knocked Out, discard this card.

Name: Copycat
Description: Shuffle your hand into your deck. Then, count the number of cards in your opponent’s hand and draw that many cards.
Supporter-Rule: You can play only one Supporter card each turn. When you play this card, put it next to your Active Pokémon. When your turn ends, discard this card.

Name: Holon Legacy
Description: Each Pokémon in play that has δ on its card (both yours and your opponent’s) has no Weakness and can’t use any Poké-Powers.
Stadium-Rule: This card stays in play when you play it. Discard this card if another Stadium card comes into play. If another card with the same name is in play, you can’t play this card.

Name: Holon Mentor
Description: Discard a card from your hand. If you can’t discard a card from your hand, you can’t play this card.Search your deck for up to 3 Basic Pokémon that each has 100 HP or less, show them to your opponent, and put them into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Supporter-Rule: You can play only one Supporter card each turn. When you play this card, put it next to your Active Pokémon. When your turn ends, discard this card.

Name: Island Hermit
Description: Choose up to 2 of your Prize cards and put them face up. (These cards remain face up for the rest of the game.) Draw 2 cards.
Supporter-Rule: You can play only one Supporter card each turn. When you play this card, put it next to your Active Pokémon. When your turn ends, discard this card.

Name: Mr. Stone’s Project
Description: Search your deck for up to 2 basic Energy cards, show them to your opponent, and put them into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward. Or, search your discard pile for up to 2 basic Energy cards, show them to your opponent, and put them into your hand.
Supporter-Rule: You can play only one Supporter card each turn. When you play this card, put it next to your Active Pokémon. When your turn ends, discard this card.

Name: Old Rod
Description: Flip 2 coins. If both are heads, search your discard pile for a Basic Pokémon or Evolution card, show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand. If both are tails, search your discard pile for a Trainer card, show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand.

Name: Professor Elm’s Training Method
Description: Search your deck for an Evolution card, show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Supporter-Rule: You can play only one Supporter card each turn. When you play this card, put it next to your Active Pokémon. When your turn ends, discard this card.

Name: Professor Oak’s Research
Description: Shuffle your hand into your deck, then draw 5 cards.
Supporter-Rule: You can play only one Supporter card each turn. When you play this card, put it next to your Active Pokémon. When your turn ends, discard this card.

Name: Strength Charm
Description: Whenever an attack from the Pokémon that Strength Charm is attached to does damage to the Active Pokémon, this attack does 10 more damage (before applying Weakness and Resistance). Discard Strength Charm at the end of the turn in which this Pokémon attacks.
Tool-Rule: Attach Strength Charm to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn’t already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it. If that Pokémon is Knocked Out, discard this card.

Name: TV Reporter
Description: Draw 3 cards. Then discard any 1 card from your hand.
Supporter-Rule: You can play only one Supporter card each turn. When you play this card, put it next to your Active Pokémon. When your turn ends, discard this card.

Name: Switch
Description: Switch 1 of your Active Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

Name: Holon Energy FF
Description: Holon Energy FF provides [C] Energy.If the Pokémon that Holon Energy FF is attached to also has a basic [R] Energy card attached to it, that Pokémon has no Weakness. If the Pokémon that Holon Energy FF is attached to also has a basic [F] Energy card attached to it, damage done by that Pokémon’s attack isn’t affected by Resistance. Ignore these effects if Holon Energy FF is attached to Pokémon-ex.

Name: Holon Energy GL
Description: Holon Energy GL provides [C] Energy.If the Pokémon that Holon Energy GL is attached to also has a basic [G] Energy card attached to it, that Pokémon can’t be affected by any Special Conditions. If the Pokémon that Holon Energy GL is attached to also has a basic [L] Energy card attached to it, damage done to that Pokémon by attacks from your opponent’s Pokémon-ex is reduced by 10. Ignore these effects if Holon Energy GL is attached to Pokémon-ex.

Name: Holon Energy WP
Description: Holon Energy WP provides [C] Energy.If the Pokémon that Holon Energy WP is attached to also has a basic [W] Energy card attached to it, prevent all effects of attacks, excluding damage, done to that Pokémon by your opponent’s Pokémon. If the Pokémon that Holon Energy WP is attached to also has a basic [P] Energy card attached to it, that Pokémon’s Retreat Cost is 0. Ignore these effects if Holon Energy WP is attached to Pokémon-ex.

Name: Boost Energy
Description: Boost Energy can be attached only to an Evolved Pokémon. Discard Boost Energy at the end of the turn it was attached. Boost Energy provides [C][C][C] Energy. The Pokémon Boost Energy is attached to can’t retreat. If the Pokémon Boost Energy is attached to isn’t an Evolved Pokémon, discard Boost Energy.

Name: δ Rainbow Energy
Description: δ Rainbow Energy provides [C] Energy. While attached to a Pokémon that has δ on its card, δ Rainbow Energy provides every type of Energy but provides only 1 Energy at a time. (Has no effect other than providing Energy.)

Name: Scramble Energy
Description: Scramble Energy can be attached only to an Evolved Pokémon (excluding Pokémon-ex). Scramble Energy provides [C] Energy. While in play, if you have more Prize cards left than your opponent, Scramble Energy provides every type of Energy but provides only 3 in any combination at a time. If the Pokémon Scramble Energy is attached to isn’t an Evolved Pokémon (or evolves into Pokémon-ex), discard Scramble Energy.

Name: Altaria ex
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may attach a basic Energy card from your hand to 1 of your Stage 2 Pokémon-ex. This power can’t be used if Altaria ex is affected by a Special Condition.
Effects: Remove 1 damage counter from each of your Pokémon.
ex-Rule: When Pokémon-ex has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Dragonite ex
Effects: Your opponent can’t play any Trainer cards (except for Supporter cards) from his or her hand during your opponent’s next turn.

Put 8 damage counters on the Defending Pokémon. If that Pokémon would be Knocked Out by this attack, put any damage counters not necessary to Knock Out the Defending Pokémon on your opponent’s Benched Pokémon in any way you like.
ex-Rule: When Pokémon-ex has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Flygon ex
Pok\u00e9-BODY-Effect: As long as Flygon ex is your Active Pokémon, put 1 damage counter on each of your opponent’s Benched Basic Pokémon between turns. You can’t use more than 1 Sand Damage Poké-Body between turns.
Effects: Does 10 damage to each of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon that has any damage counters on it. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
ex-Rule: When Pokémon-ex has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Gardevoir ex
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), if Gardevoir ex is your Active Pokémon, you may put an Imprison marker on 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon. Any Pokémon that has any Imprison markers on it can’t use any Poké-Powers or Poké-Bodies. This power can’t be used if Gardevoir ex is affected by a Special Condition.
Effects: You may move a [R] Energy card attached to Gardevoir ex to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
ex-Rule: When Pokémon-ex has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Kingdra ex
Pok\u00e9-BODY-Effect: Any damage done to your Stage 2 Pokémon-ex by your opponent’s attacks is reduced by 10 (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Effects: Search your discard pile for an Energy card and attach it to Kingdra ex.
Kingdra ex has no Weakness during your opponent’s next turn.
ex-Rule: When Pokémon-ex has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Latias ex
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may attach a basic Energy card from your hand to your Latias, Latias ex, Latios, or Latios ex. If you do, your turn ends. This power can’t be used if Latias ex is affected by a Special Condition.
Effects: If the Defending Pokémon is Knocked Out by this attack, discard 2 [R] Energy attached to Latias ex.
ex-Rule: When Pokémon-ex has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Latios ex
Pok\u00e9-BODY-Effect: The Retreat Cost for each of your Latias, Latias ex, Latios, and Latios ex is 0.
Effects: Prevent all effects of attack, including damage, done to Latios ex by your opponent’s Pokémon-ex during your opponent’s next turn.
ex-Rule: When Pokémon-ex has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Rayquaza ex
Pok\u00e9-BODY-Effect: If you have more Prize cards left than your opponent, ignore all [C] Energy necessary to use Rayquaza ex’s Special Circuit and Sky-high Claws attacks.
Effects: Choose 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon. This attack does 30 damage to that Pokémon. If you choose a Pokémon that has any Poké-Powers or Poké-Bodies, this attack does 50 damage instead. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
ex-Rule: When Pokémon-ex has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Salamence ex
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may use this power. Salamence ex’s type is [R] until the end of your turn.
Effects: You may do 40 damage instead of 80 to the Defending Pokémon. If you do, this attack does 40 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
ex-Rule: When Pokémon-ex has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Tyranitar ex
Effects: Put a Shock-wave marker on 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon.
If the Defending Pokémon is a Stage 2 Evolved Pokémon, this attack does 70 damage plus 20 more damage.
Choose 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon that has any Shock-wave markers on it. That Pokémon is Knocked Out.
ex-Rule: When Pokémon-ex has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Charizard Star
Effects: You may discard an Energy card attached to Charizard Star. If you do, search your discard pile for an Energy card (excluding the one you discarded) and attach it to Charizard Star.
Discard all Energy cards attached to Charizard Star and discard the top 3 cards from your opponent’s deck.
Star-Rule: You can’t have more than 1 Pokémon Star in your deck.

Name: Mew Star
Effects: Choose an attack on 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon in play. Mimicry copies that attack. This attack does nothing if Mew Star doesn’t have the Energy necessary to use that attack. (You must still do anything else required for that attack.) Mew Star performs that attack.

Choose 1 basic Energy card attached to Mew Star. This attack does 20 damage to each of your opponent’s Pokémon that is the same type as the basic Energy card that you chose. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Star-Rule: You can’t have more than 1 Pokémon Star in your deck.
